findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 1995 of 1995 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 13302 of 13302 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

findings.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
findings.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and any elevators, with a level for each.
findings.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt.
findings.why: Trip planners can't guide riders through the station, and there is no step-free route information for wheelchair users.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_phone_number' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Invalid phone number (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_route' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for route (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Pick a darker/lighter route_text_color for the flagged routes.
findings.what: Some route colors don't contrast with their text color.
findings.why: Route badges become unreadable, especially for riders with low vision.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unused_station' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unused station (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 7 kinds of issue across 578 instances (2 error, 574 warning, 2 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 121 days.

findings.fix: Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The trip updates realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check the vehicle positions endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The vehicle positions realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The service alerts realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 143 of 143 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 9 times: 0 of 3 feeds healthy; 0.0% of scheduled trips had live predictions.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 122 days.
States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 1 validator error to resolve. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 122 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed is close to NTD-ready. 1 validator error to resolve.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 25 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (195, 282, 289, 291, 292, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 1936 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Temple Ave and Diamond Bar Blvd E", "Orange Grove Ave and Glen Ave N", "Golden Springs Rd and Calbourne Dr E", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

recommendations.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
recommendations.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and elevators, and levels.txt for each floor, so the step-free route is described.
recommendations.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt and levels.txt.
recommendations.why: Without pathways and levels, a wheelchair user can't tell whether a step-free route exists inside the station, and trip planners can't route anyone through it.
